---
title: Global residualization
jupyter: python3
---



### Set up and get data

```{python}
## Import modules
import os
import abct
import numpy as np
from scipy import io
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.subplots as sp

## Unpack data
data = io.loadmat(os.path.join(".", "data", "hcp_data.mat"))

# Downsampled activity for fast computation
activity = data["activity"]
coactivity = activity.T @ activity
```

### Compute variants of global residualization

```{python}
## Residual structural networks
connectivity = data["connectivity"]
idx = ~np.eye(len(connectivity), dtype=bool) 

conn = dict()
conn["Degree-corrected"] = abct.residualn(connectivity, "degree")[idx]
conn["First-component-removed"] = abct.residualn(connectivity, "rankone")[idx]

# Regress global signal and normalize to unit norm
activity_gsr = abct.residualn(activity.T, "global").T
activity_gsr = activity_gsr / np.linalg.norm(activity_gsr, axis=0, keepdims=True)
coactivity_gsr = activity_gsr.T @ activity_gsr

coact = dict()
coact["Degree-corrected"] = abct.residualn(coactivity, "degree")[idx]
coact["Global-signal-regressed"] = coactivity_gsr[idx]
```

### Visualize residual structural networks

```{python}
# Create a scatter plot of degree-corrected and rank-one subtracted structural networks
r = np.corrcoef(conn["First-component-removed"], conn["Degree-corrected"])[0, 1]
fig = px.scatter(
    x=conn["First-component-removed"],
    y=conn["Degree-corrected"],
    labels={"x": "Link weights after first-component removal", "y": "Link weights after degree correction"},
    title=f"Connectivity (r = {r:.3f})",
    width=600, height=600,
)
fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5).show()
```

### Visualize residual co-activity networks

```{python}
# Create a scatter plot of degree-corrected and global-signal regressed coactivity
r = np.corrcoef(coact["Global-signal-regressed"], coact["Degree-corrected"])[0, 1]
fig = px.scatter(
    x=coact["Global-signal-regressed"],
    y=coact["Degree-corrected"],
    labels={"x": "Link weights after global-signal regression", "y": "Link weights after degree correction"},
    title=f"Co-activity (r = {r:.3f})",
    width=600, height=600,
)
fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5).show()
```
